seoTitle: Print a Customer Statement in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: See how to print a customer statement in QuickBooks Online to summarize outstanding balances and keep clients informed about what they owe.
intro: Customer statements give clients a clear snapshot of what they owe across multiple invoices, which speeds up collections and cuts down on billing confusion. This guide explains how to generate and print a customer statement in QuickBooks Online. It's useful for small business owners and bookkeepers who want a professional, consistent way to communicate account balances.

steps.heading: Open the customer statement feature
steps.body: Navigate to the customers area in QuickBooks Online and locate the option to create a statement.

steps.heading: Choose statement settings
steps.body: Select the statement type — such as balance forward or open item — and the date range you want the statement to cover.

steps.heading: Customize the statement details
steps.body: Adjust formatting or messaging on the statement so it matches your business's branding and communicates clearly to the customer.

steps.heading: Print or send the statement
steps.body: Once the statement looks correct, print it directly or export it to send electronically to the customer.

steps.heading: Track follow-up
steps.body: Use the statement as a reference point when following up on overdue balances to keep your accounts receivable organized.

faq.question: Can I send customer statements by email instead of printing?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online lets you email statements directly to customers in addition to printing them.

faq.question: What's the difference between a balance forward and open item statement?
faq.answer: A balance forward statement shows a running total, while an open item statement lists each unpaid invoice individually.

faq.question: Can I generate statements for multiple customers at once?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online allows batch statement creation so you don't have to generate them one at a time.

faq.question: Will a statement replace the need to send invoices?
faq.answer: No, statements summarize existing invoices rather than replace them — they're best used as a follow-up reminder.
